rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE IRENE LIEBERMAN REVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Expunge Lis Pendens
rulings.Rulings: The motion to expunge lis pendens is granted. The underlying petition is not a real property claim with respect to the two subject properties. Petitioners do not allege a basis on which they have a right to ownership or possession. At most, even assuming they have adequately referred to the properties in their petition, they only allege a basis on which the administrator of the estate--not themselves--would have those rights. In any event, Petitioners have not carried their burden of showing a probability of success on the merits. They cannot shift the burden of persuasion as to undue influence to Respondent because he is a close relative (Probate Code section 21382), and they have not shown that Respondent participated meaningfully in procuring the trust or that he would benefit unduly under the trust (see Hernandez v. Kieferle (2011) 200 Ca.App.4th 419, 431). In any event, Respondent's evidence at this stage shows that the trust was not the product of "excessive persuasion ... overcoming [trustor's] free will [that] results in inequity." See Welf. & Inst. Code section 15610.70(a); Probate Code section 86. The evidence presented on this motion shows trustor was not a vulnerable victim; she was not likely to succumb to Respondent's authority; Respondent did not intimidate, coerce, isolate, rush, or control trustor; and the trust's provisions are not inequitable given all the circumstances of the parties' relationships. See id. Respondent's objections to paragraph 4 of the Declaration of Paula Lieberman and paragraph 4 of the Decalaration of David Lieberman are sustained as lacking personal knowledge and as hearsay. Respondent's request for attorney's fees, not having been raised in the Notice of Motion, is denied. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:30 p.m. Tuesday, March 22, 2016.
